essence: Supports public health by inspecting environments, identifying hazards, and ensuring safe living and working conditions.

identity.title: Environmental Health Technician
identity.summary: Assists environmental health officers by inspecting premises, monitoring hazards, collecting samples, and supporting public‑health enforcement.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in food safety, housing standards, or environmental monitoring can exceed £40k–£50k+.

qualifications: Environmental health or public‑health support training
Knowledge of food hygiene, housing standards, and environmental hazards
Ability to collect samples and complete inspection reports
Understanding of health and safety legislation

lifestyle.description: Environmental health technicians support inspections of food businesses, rental properties, workplaces, and public spaces. They collect samples, check hygiene standards, investigate complaints, and help enforce regulations. The role involves travel, public interaction, and detailed reporting. It suits individuals who enjoy investigative work, community impact, and ensuring safe environments.

habits: Follows strict sampling and hygiene procedures
Communicates clearly with businesses and the public
Monitors compliance with health and safety standards
